_doc: DC1 numeric puzzle codes that share ONE common scramble function: a single 4-digit keypad code table baked into DINO.exe .data (AOT-recompiled from the JP build, 8 identical copies) checked by a shared comparator, with an optional in-prose document that states the code as a glyph-token string. A randomizer scrambles a row by (a) writing the new digits to all 8 EXE copies and (b) rewriting the document glyph run to the same number. Decoded + CE-witnessed 2026-07-01/02; see docs/dc1/MGMT-OFFICE-SAFE-PUZZLE-DECODE.md 14/16/17.

keypad_code_table.region_select: comparator picks US half if byte[[PSX-scratch0]+0x9ab8] & 0x80 else JP half; this build runs JP

document_glyph_format.normal_glyph: (index << 1) | 0x8000  (high byte 0x80, low byte = index*2, always even)
document_glyph_format.language_independent: glyph indices identical across english/ french/ etc.; only the text differs
document_glyph_format.location: in the decompressed room RDT (type-7 LZSS entry, load 0x80100000); edit needs re-LZSS + package repack

family.note: no readable document mention found (Reverse% content)
no readable document mention found (Reverse% content)

excluded_other_formats._doc: Detected by the same scan but NOT part of the keypad-table common function; a randomizer for those needs a different lever.

id_card_fingerprint_panels.format: 5-key/2-row object type 0x1a, RAW-digit encoding (no +1), separate comparators 0x800eddc8/0x800edf98

key_chip_codes.format: 4-digit code shown in a document but the lock is a tile/slide puzzle, not the keypad table
